prd.title: macOS Port — Cross-Platform Expansion to Apple Silicon + Intel Darwin

stories.title: Validate GPUI rev 0b984b5 builds on aarch64-apple-darwin
Resolve core-graphics transitive version conflict if it manifests
stories.resolution: Cancelled per AC4. Cargo.lock inspection confirms core-graphics 0.23.2 (via zed-scap -> cocoa 0.25.0) and 0.24.0 (via GPUI + cocoa 0.26.0) coexist but occupy distinct crate symbol namespaces per Rust's standard mangling — no link collision expected. The PRD's prescribed patch (core-graphics = "0.24" in [patch.crates-io]) would not resolve because cocoa 0.25.0 pins ^0.23; a real remediation would require a patch-source redirect or a zed-scap bump, both out of scope. Reopen this story only if the US-001 CI run on macos-14 produces duplicate-symbol link errors. Finding documented in tasks/spike-macos-build.md (Addendum).

stories.title: Install Xcode Command Line Tools + verify gpui_macos deps compile
IPC socket path fallback via $TMPDIR on macOS
cwd_now() macOS implementation via proc_pidinfo
stories.resolution: Implemented via libc::proc_pidinfo (already in dep tree) rather than the libproc crate specified in AC1. Reason: libproc 0.14.11 does not expose a PIDInfo wrapper struct for flavor 9 (VNodePathInfo) and its own pidcwd helper is explicitly unimplemented on macOS — going through libproc would require a local wrapper-struct shim for no added value. The semantic requirement (proc_pidinfo with PROC_PIDVNODEPATHINFO) is preserved and documented in the fn docstring. Runtime verification (AC2/AC4) deferred to macos-14 hardware — Linux regression (AC5) unaffected.

stories.title: detect_ports() macOS implementation via libproc
stories.resolution: Implemented via libproc 0.14 (macOS-only dep per AC1) for per-PID FD + socket introspection, paired with libc::proc_listchildpids for descendant walking. Mirrors the Linux structure: expand workspace PIDs to all descendants, enumerate Socket FDs, filter to TCP Listen state, extract port from insi_lport (network byte order, low 16 bits of c_int). AC6 defer path NOT taken — pipeline default is to implement. Runtime verification (AC2/AC3) deferred to macos-14 hardware.

stories.title: Migrate app-global keybindings to secondary modifier
Add Cmd+C / Cmd+V terminal copy/paste bindings on macOS
Minimal native menu bar via cx.set_menus
Generate PaneFlow.icns from existing PNG assets
stories.resolution: AC3 deviation: Linux fallback chain is iconutil (macOS) -> png2icns -> icnsutil -> python3 inline packer. Added python3 packer (stdlib only, ~25 lines) because neither png2icns nor icnsutil was installable on the dev host; ImageMagick 7 on the host ships without an ICNS coder. Output validated: file(1) reports 'Mac OS X icon', all 10 typed chunks (icp4, ic11, icp5, ic12, ic07, ic13, ic08, ic14, ic09, ic10) parse cleanly and each carries a valid PNG payload. iconutil path on macOS runners is unchanged and remains the primary. Visual audit of 1024px upscale deferred to PR reviewer on macOS (AC5).

stories.title: Code signing + notarization pipeline in release.yml
stories.resolution: Implemented as two self-contained scripts (scripts/sign-macos.sh, scripts/notarize-macos.sh) plus two dormant steps in .github/workflows/release.yml gated by `if: runner.os == 'macOS'`. The steps remain inert on today's ubuntu-22.04 matrix legs (AC7) and activate the moment US-019 adds the macos-14 leg that calls scripts/bundle-macos.sh first. AC2/3/4/5/6 logic implemented and syntax-validated; real execution requires Apple secrets + macOS runner and is deferred to US-019 CI. Secrets to populate in repo settings before first macOS release: APPLE_DEVELOPER_CERT_P12, APPLE_DEVELOPER_CERT_PASSWORD, APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID.

stories.title: .dmg creation + upload to GitHub Releases
stories.resolution: Implemented via hand-rolled hdiutil + osascript (AC1 permitted either approach; hdiutil avoids an npm install step on the macOS runner). New script scripts/create-dmg.sh produces `dist/paneflow-<version>-<arch>-apple-darwin.dmg` with a drag-to-Applications Finder layout driven by AppleScript (660x400 window, icons at {165,200} and {495,200}, background asset at .background/background.png). Filename format matches US-008's AssetFormat::Dmg matcher exactly. AC3 enforced by a post-convert remount + codesign --verify --deep --strict inside the script. release.yml extended with two macOS-gated steps (produce-dmg + stage-dmg-into-release-assets); dormant on today's Linux matrix, activates when US-019 adds the macos-14 leg. US-019 deferred sub-concern: the existing Linux 'Stage release assets' step isn't gated to runner.os == 'Linux' yet; US-019 must add that guard when introducing the macos leg. Visual/Gatekeeper verification (AC2/AC3/AC5) deferred to macOS CI in US-019.

stories.title: Homebrew cask formula + self-hosted tap
stories.resolution: Implemented via two artifacts in this repo: (1) packaging/homebrew/paneflow.rb — canonical arch-aware cask template with version/sha256/url/app/depends_on/zap stanzas per AC2, (2) .github/workflows/update-cask.yml — on release: published, downloads both .dmg assets, computes per-arch SHA256s, renders Casks/paneflow.rb via sed-based substitution of the canonical template, and pushes to the external tap repo. Operator setup (manual, out-of-band): (a) create public repo ArthurDEV44/homebrew-paneflow with empty Casks/ dir, (b) create a fine-grained PAT with contents: read-and-write on that repo, (c) add HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret to this repo. Workflow will error clearly with setup instructions if the secret is absent. AC3/AC5/AC6 runtime brew behaviour is satisfied structurally by DSL conformance; verification deferred to first post-US-019 release when both matrix legs produce .dmg artifacts.

stories.title: macOS CI check job in ci.yml
stories.resolution: Extended the US-001-era macos-check job with three AC2-mandated gates (cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --workspace --target aarch64-apple-darwin -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace --target aarch64-apple-darwin) and added a dorny/paths-filter@v3-powered `changes` detection job that short-circuits macos-check on docs-only / non-workflow .github/** PRs (AC5). Filter scope: src-app/**/*.rs, crates/**/*.rs, **/Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, .github/workflows/ci.yml — the last entry ensures CI-file PRs exercise their own validation. Clippy scoped to --target aarch64-apple-darwin so platform-gated modules (runtime_paths.rs, InstallMethod::AppBundle, AssetFormat::Dmg, MACOS_ONLY_DEFAULTS) are type-checked on macOS rather than only on Linux. rustfmt+clippy components added to the dtolnay/rust-toolchain step. AC3 rust-cache already present (key aarch64-apple-darwin). Ubuntu check job unchanged — still runs on every event.

stories.title: Release.yml macos-14 matrix leg for aarch64 .dmg
stories.resolution: Extended the release.yml build matrix with two new legs: macos-14 (aarch64-apple-darwin, mandatory) and macos-13 (x86_64-apple-darwin, best-effort via continue-on-error: true at the matrix entry, lifted through to the job via `continue-on-error: ${{ matrix.continue-on-error == true }}`). Gated 11 Linux-only steps (apt install, metainfo validate, cargo-deb/generate-rpm installs, tar.gz/deb/Lintian/rpm/AppImage production, GPG sign, Linux staging) with `if: runner.os == 'Linux'`. Added 2 macOS-only prelude steps before the US-015/016 dormant ones: Xcode CLT + Metal toolchain verify (mirrored from ci.yml), and scripts/bundle-macos.sh invocation. Version argument to bundle-macos.sh and create-dmg.sh uses ${TAG#v} (bare semver per Apple CFBundleShortVersionString requirements and matching US-008's documented .dmg filename convention). Upload artifact name disambiguated with runner.os suffix (release-assets-<arch>-<runner.os>); both smoke-test jobs updated to download release-assets-x86_64-Linux. Release publish job's release-assets-* glob unchanged. AC4 wall-time measurement (<30 min) deferred to first live run.
